summary: Grocery delivery app — a mobile/web application for ordering groceries online and having them delivered.
imageRequirements:
## Required Images
- Bright, appetizing hero banner with an assortment of fresh colorful vegetables and fruits on a light background with warm green accents (hero-banner.dim_1200x400.png)
- Category tile illustration for Fruits & Vegetables — a colorful flat-style produce basket (cat-fruits-veggies.dim_400x300.png)
- Category tile illustration for Dairy — minimalist flat-style milk bottle and cheese (cat-dairy.dim_400x300.png)
- Category tile illustration for Bakery — warm flat-style bread loaf and pastries (cat-bakery.dim_400x300.png)
- Category tile illustration for Beverages — flat-style juice bottles and a coffee cup (cat-beverages.dim_400x300.png)
- Category tile illustration for Snacks — flat-style chips bag and mixed snack items (cat-snacks.dim_400x300.png)
- App logo mark — a simple, modern leaf-and-cart icon in green tones (logo-icon.dim_128x128.png)

existing_features.title: Design and apply a fresh, vibrant visual theme inspired by farmers' markets and fresh produce — using warm greens, earthy neutrals, and crisp whites as the color palette, with clean sans-serif typography, card-based layouts, and subtle produce-inspired accent illustrations. Apply this theme consistently across all pages.

Build a homepage with a hero banner, a featured categories section, and a 'Featured Products' grid showing top products. Include a persistent navigation bar with the app name/logo, a search bar, and a cart icon showing the current item count.

Build a product browse/catalog page that displays all products in a responsive grid. Support filtering by category and sorting by price (low-to-high, high-to-low). Each product card must show name, image, price, and an 'Add to Cart' button.

Implement a client-side shopping cart (stored in React state) that allows users to add products, increase/decrease quantities, remove items, and view a running subtotal. Display the cart as a slide-in drawer accessible from the cart icon in the navbar.

Build a checkout page where users enter their name, delivery address, and a delivery time preference. On submission, call the backend to place the order and show an order confirmation screen with an order summary and estimated delivery message.

Build a simple admin/orders page (accessible via a nav link) that fetches and displays all placed orders in a table, showing order ID, buyer name, delivery address, item count, total amount, and status.
Render the generated app hero banner image and category/product placeholder images as static assets from frontend/public/assets/generated.

new_feature_requests.summary: Create a backend data model and persistence layer for grocery products, including fields: id, name, category, description, price (in cents), imageUrl, stock quantity, and availability flag.
Seed the backend with at least 20 sample grocery products across at least 5 categories (e.g., Fruits & Vegetables, Dairy, Bakery, Beverages, Snacks).

Create a backend order model and persistence layer. An order must include: orderId, buyerName, deliveryAddress, items (list of productId + quantity), totalAmount, status (pending/confirmed/delivered), and createdAt timestamp. Expose a function to place a new order and a function to retrieve all orders.
